category_sources: Official GitHub README describes MCP Tools as a CLI for working with Model Context Protocol servers.
source_facts.description says this is a CLI for interacting with MCP servers using stdio and HTTP transport.
display_name_sources: Official GitHub README uses the heading MCP Tools and describes it as a Swiss Army Knife for MCP Servers.
docs_sources: Official GitHub README includes getting started, features, commands, transports, server aliases, and server modes.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo is already set to https://github.com/f/mcptools; per instruction, repo is returned as null and not second-guessed.
tags_sources: Official GitHub README lists tools/resources/prompts commands, mock server, proxy, guard, and project scaffolding features.
source_facts.description mentions MCP servers and stdio/HTTP transport.
